data.description:
# Cut the v0.8.0 release (minor bump)

Cut the next release of `nava-rebar`, incrementing the **minor** version `0.7.1 -> 0.8.0`,
using the new dispatch-from-`main` release-authorization workflow (story fd84). Bump the
three version manifests in lockstep, promote the curated CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` section
to `[0.8.0]`, land the bump on `main` through the Gerrit two-vote gate, then dispatch
`release.yml` from `main` with `version=0.8.0` and drive it to a completed publish.

## Why

`main` is at `0.7.1`; the accumulated user-facing changes in `CHANGELOG.md` `[Unreleased]`
(new `idea` status, code-review eval arm, criteria overlays, session-id resolver, removed
back-compat shims, etc.) warrant a release. Pre-1.0 SemVer permits breaking changes in a
minor bump, so `0.8.0` is correct. The new `release.yml` only publishes from a
`workflow_dispatch`-on-`main` run whose `authorize` job asserts version-lockstep, so the
manifests must equal the dispatch `version` input on `main` before the run.

## What / Scope

- `pyproject.toml` — `[project].version` `0.7.1` -> `0.8.0`.
- `server.json` — top-level `version` and `packages[0].version` -> `0.8.0`.
- `CHANGELOG.md` — retitle `## [Unreleased]` to `## [0.8.0] - 2026-07-16` and open a fresh
  empty `## [Unreleased]` above it (Keep a Changelog release step; the section is already
  hand-curated so no `git-cliff` regeneration is needed).
- Land the one commit on `main` via Gerrit (`LLM-Review +1` AND `Verified +1`), then dispatch
  `release.yml --ref main -f version=0.8.0`, approve the `pypi` required-reviewer gate, and
  monitor to completion. No workflow/code logic changes — this is a release cut.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pyproject.toml` `[project].version == "0.8.0"` (verify: `grep '^version' pyproject.toml`).
- [ ] `server.json` top-level `version` and every `packages[].version` == `"0.8.0"` (verify:
  `python -c "import json;d=json.load(open('server.json'));assert d['version']=='0.8.0' and all(p['version']=='0.8.0' for p in d['packages'])"`).
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` has a `## [0.8.0] - 2026-07-16` section carrying the promoted entries and
  a fresh empty `## [Unreleased]` above it (verify: `grep -n '## \[0.8.0\]' CHANGELOG.md`).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass locally, and `python -m build && python -m twine check dist/*` succeed on the bumped tree.
- [ ] [operator-attested] The bump commit lands on `main` through the Gerrit two-vote gate
  (`LLM-Review +1` AND `Verified +1`) — attested by recording the Gerrit change URL + both
  vote outcomes/timestamps as a comment on this ticket.
- [ ] `release.yml` is dispatched from `main` with `version=0.8.0`; the `authorize` job passes
  all four guards and the run completes with `publish`, `github_release`, and `mcp_registry`
  all successful — attested by the workflow run URL + job conclusions recorded on this ticket.
- [ ] PyPI serves `nava-rebar 0.8.0` (verify: `curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://pypi.org/pypi/nava-rebar/0.8.0/json` == 200).
- [ ] A GitHub Release `v0.8.0` (marked Latest, sdist+wheel attached) and tag exist (verify:
  `gh release view v0.8.0 --repo navapbc/rebar`), and the MCP registry lists `0.8.0` (verify:
  the registry search returns version 0.8.0) — recorded as a comment on this ticket.
data.title: Cut the v0.8.0 release (minor bump)
